meta.title: Nakafurano Kita-Hoshi Ski Area
meta.description: Escape Furano crowds—Tokachi views and untracked powder from ¥1,030 adult day ticket at a local hill.

hero.title:
Kita-Hoshi
Ski Area
hero.tagline: The lavender hill becomes a powder hideout in winter—a Furano mega-resort spin-off.

transit.title:
15 minutes on foot
from Nakafurano Station.
transit.lead: For riders skipping Furano Ski Resort crowds and ¥8,000 day tickets—one single chair, one pitch, but fresh tracks in the untracked zone and a mega-resort-grade panorama.
transit.imgAlt: Panorama of Furano basin and Tokachi peaks from Kita-Hoshi

primary.title: Mega-resort-grade views
primary.lead: Tokachi peaks and the Furano basin from the summit—compact hill, panorama that rivals the big resort.
primary.imgAlt: Tokachi panorama from Kita-Hoshi Ski Area

secondary.title: Hidden untracked powder
secondary.lead: ~40% of the run left ungroomed—fresh tracks on powder mornings with near-private feel in the Furano area.

pass.title:
Adults ¥1,030,
children ¥510.
pass.lead: A fraction of Furano Ski Resort pricing—local municipal rates for short powder sessions and night laps.
pass.noticeBody: No restaurant on the hill—use Popura Farm, town eateries, or Manpuku no Yu nearby.

rental.title:
Ski, then
onsen and sweets.
rental.lead: Morning powder at Kita-Hoshi, lunch at Popura Farm, afternoon at Manpuku no Yu—a spin-off loop that lifts spend per visitor.
rental.whisper: Differentiate from Farm Tomita and Furano Ski Resort via crowd avoidance, low price, and views (report).
rental.imgAlt: Onsen and cafés in Nakafurano—post-ski loop

yukinko.title:
Night skiing
after work.
yukinko.lead: Night skiing on weekdays and Saturdays until ~20:10–20:30—short focused laps between Furano sightseeing or after work.

family.title:
Sled & tube
snow-play area.
family.lead: Dedicated snow-play zone with sleds and tubes—not only alpine skiing—a light snow experience for Furano day-trip visitors.
family.noticeBody: Single chair can feel daunting for young children riding alone—staff assist at load/unload (well reviewed).

spa.title:
After skiing,
Banka no Yu awaits.
spa.lead: Furano Latteur Banka no Yu and Poplar Farm—ski Kita-Hoshi in the morning, then loop through town for onsen and sweets.
spa.whisper: No restaurant on the hill—pair with nearby Furano town stops.

workation.title:
Morning powder,
afternoon in town.
workation.lead: Skip all-day mega-resort laps—2–3 hours on fresh untracked snow, then onsen and lavender-related sights—a half-day spin-off model.

local.title: Furano spin-off guide
local.body: Honest LP for riders avoiding Furano Ski Resort crowds and prices—low cost, space, untracked snow, views (report ch. 5).

schools.title: Kita-Hoshi lavender brand
schools.body: Summer lavender garden and winter ski hill—one Mt. Kita-Hoshi brand for year-round digital touchpoints (report ch. 6).

summer.title: Lavender in summer, powder in winter
summer.body: Leverage lavender garden summer awareness for winter skiing—#FuranoOffPeak hashtag strategy (report).

access.note: ~15 min walk from JR Nakafurano Station · free parking
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Asahikawa Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Furano, Biei, and Sounkyo drives
